LAW AND MOTION 301, ARGUED AND PLAINTIFFS CALIFORNIA ADVOCATES FOR NURSING HOME REFORM, PATRICIA BRYANT AND JULIE FUDGE'S MOTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS GRANTED. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 12, 2006 SO THAT THE PARTIES MAY JOINTLY ADDRESS REMEDIAL ASPECTS OF THE WRIT FOR THE ORDER WITH CLEAR INTERIM STEPS FOR GETTING TO FULL COMPLIANCE. IF THE PARTIES CANNOT AGREE ON A PROPOSAL, A JOINT STATEMENT IS TO BE SUBMITTED TO THE COURT. THE JOINT STATEMENT IS DUE PURSUANT TO STATUTE. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, COUNSEL UPDATED THE COURT ON PROGRESS TOWARDS PREPARING A FORM OF ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFFS CALIFORNIA ADVOCATES FOR NURSING HOME REFORM, PATRICIA BRYANT AND JULIE FUDGE'S WRIT OF MANDATE AND ADDRESSING STEPS TO BRING RESPONDENTS TO FULL COMPLIANCE. THE COURT PUT ON THE RECORD ITS THOUGHTS ON A 4 TO 8 MONTH PLAN, AND ADDRESSED SOME ISSUES ON WHICH NO AGREEMENT HAS BEEN REACHED. IF THE PARTIES STILL CANNOT REACH AN AGREEMENT ON A FORM OF ORDER, IF NECESSARY, THE PARTIES WILL SUBMIT PROPOSED FORMS OF ORDER WITH BRIEF LETTERS OF EXPLANATION TO THE COURT FOR ITS CONSIDERATION. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONERS CALIFORNIA ADVOCATES FOR NURSING HOME REFORM, PATRICIA BRYANT AND JULIE FUDGE'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IS GRANTED PER CCP 1021.5. 1) ACTION RESULTED IN ENFORCEMENT OF IMPORTANT RIGHT AFFECTING PUBLIC INTEREST; 2) SIGNIFICANT BENEFIT CONFERRED ON GENERAL PUBLIC; 3) PRIVATE ENFORCEMENT IS THE ONLY MEANS OF ENFORCING LAW AGAINST DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES; THEREFORE, $283,207.00 TO LODESTAR IS REASONABLE. THE COURT FINDS THE MULTIPLIER OF 1.25 REASONABLE. THE CASE IS NOT LENGTHY OR VASTLY COMPLEX. TOTAL AMOUNT IS AS FOLLOWS: $203,207 TIMES 1.25 PLUS 49,089 IN COSTS EQUALS $403,098. ORDER SIGNED. (301/REQ/JF) JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; REPORTER: BELDON YEE, CSR # 2647

RULING:
A R G U E D; PETITIONERS CALIFORNIA ADVOCATES FOR NURSING HOME REFORM, PATRICIA BRYANT and JULIE FUDGE'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS IS Granted per CCP 1021.5. 1) Action resulted in enforcement of important right affecting public interest; 2) Significant benefit conferred on general public; 3) Private enforcement is the only means of enforcing law against Department of Health Services; therefore, $283,207.00 to Lodestar is reasonable. The Court finds the multiplier of 1.25 reasonable. The case is not lengthy or vastly complex. Total amount is as follows: $203,207 times 1.25 plus 49,089 in costs equals $403,098. Order signed. (301/REQ/jf) Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Reporter: Beldon Yee, CSR # 2647
